[[Sam Burgess]] (brother) }} }} '''Thomas Burgess''' (born 21 April 1992) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League|English Super League]]. He has played for the [[England Knights]], [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level. Burgess previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and has played for the [[NRL All Stars team|World All Stars]]. He played in the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|Rabbitohs]] [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] winning team alongside his twin brother [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George Burgess]], and older brother [[Sam Burgess]]. Burgess is also the younger brother of retired rugby league footballer [[Luke Burgess (rugby league)|Luke Burgess]]. ==Background== Burgess was born on 21 April 1992 in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire, England]]. His father, Mark Burgess who died of [[Motor neurone disease]], was also a [[rugby league]] footballer who played for [[Mansfield Marksman|Nottingham City]], [[Rochdale Hornets]], [[Dewsbury Rams]] and [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]];{{cite news|last=Irvine|first=Christopher|newspaper=The Times Online|url=http://www.timesonline.co.uk/tol/sport/rugby_league/article2741406.ece|title=Sam Burgess inspired by memory of late father|date=26 October 2007|access-date=17 September 2009}}{{dead link|date=September 2024|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}} while his mother Julie is a teacher, currently employed at [[The Scots College]] in [[Bellevue Hill, New South Wales|Bellevue Hill]], Sydney.{{cite news|work= Daily Telegraph|title=Meet the magnificent Mrs Burgess|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/entertainment/sydney-confidential/meet-the-magnificent-mrs-burgess/story-fni0cvc9-1226690449682}} Burgess is brother of [[Luke Burgess (rugby league)|Luke]], and fellow [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] players [[Sam Burgess|Sam]], and twin-brother [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]].{{cite news|publisher=Halifax Evening Courier|url=http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/local/Bulls-snap-up-Burgess-twins.3682386.jp|title=Bulls snap up Burgess twins|date=17 January 2008|access-date=17 September 2009}} Burgess attended [[Castle Hall Academy]] to receive a secondary education before becoming a professional [[rugby league]] footballer. He played his junior rugby league for [[Dewsbury Moor]].{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10374182/Burgess-brothers-are-back-on-family-business-for-England-in-World-Cup.html|title=Burgess brothers are back on family business for England in World Cup|date=11 October 2013|work=Telegraph.co.uk}} Burgess was a product of the [[Leeds Rhinos]] junior development system, before moving onto the Bradford Bulls. ==Playing career== ===2011=== Burgess first featured in three of the four pre-season friendlies of the [[2011 Bradford Bulls season|2011 season]], playing against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], the [[Dewsbury Rams]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He made his [[Super League]] début in Round 8 against the [[London Broncos]] off the interchange bench in the Bradford Bulls' 24-22 win.{{cite web|work= Sky Sports|title=Bulls hold off Pryce bid|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150104014438/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,12212_3343394,00.html|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,19936,12212_3343394,00.html|archive-date=4 January 2015}} In Round 20 against the [[Catalans Dragons]], Burgess scored his first Super League try in the Bradford Bulls' 34-28 loss.{{cite web|work= Sky Sports|title=Catalans humble Bulls|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150104014647/http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,12213_3406247,00+en-USS_01DBC.html|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,12213_3406247,00+en-USS_01DBC.html|archive-date=4 January 2015}} Burgess also played in both [[Challenge Cup]] games against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Wigan Warriors]], scoring against Halifax. He played in 20 matches and scored 2 tries in 2011. ===2012=== On 6 August, it was announced that Burgess had signed a one-year deal with the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] for 2013, joining brothers [[Sam Burgess|Sam]], [[Luke Burgess (rugby league)|Luke]] and [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]].{{cite news|work= Sydney Morning Herald|title=Burgess boys to pack in for Souths|author=Glenn Jackson|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/burgess-boys-to-pack-in-for-souths-20120806-23qcj.html}} Burgess scored one try from 25 matches for the season. He was selected in the [[England Knights]] squad for the [[2012 European Cup (rugby league)|2012 European Cup]]. Burgess made his Knights début in the 56–4 win over Ireland scoring a try in the process. In this competition he also featured in the 62–24 win over Scotland. [[File:George Burgess, Sam Burgess & Tom Burgess 2013.jpg|thumb|right|Tom Burgess with older brother [[Sam Burgess|Sam]], and twin brother [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]] in 2013.]] ===2013=== In Round 15, Burgess made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] début for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]] against the [[Parramatta Eels]], playing off the interchange bench in the 30-10 win at [[ANZ Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/south-sydney-rabbitohs-beats-parramatte-eels-to-go-three-clear-on-the-nrl-ladder/story-fn2mcuj6-1226668400742|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs beats Parramatte Eels to go three clear on the NRL ladder|author=Adrian Warren|date=8 March 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=9 March 2016}} In August, against the [[Wests Tigers]], the Burgess Brothers became the first set of four brothers to line up in the same Australian side since Ray, Roy, Rex and Bernard Norman played for Sydney's Annandale club in the 1910 NSWRFL season. South Sydney won 32-18 at [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|SFS]].{{cite news|work= Daily Telegraph|title=South Sydney rabbitohs leave it late to run down Wests Tigers in Benji Marshall's 200th NRL game|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-leave-it-late-to-run-down-wests-tigers-in-benji-marshalls-200th-nrl-game/story-fni3fbgz-1226707869520}} Burgess finished his début year in the NRL with 9 appearances. He joined [[England national rugby league team|England]]'s 24-man squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|World Cup]],{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/three-burgess-brothers-named-in-england-world-cup-squad-20131001-2uqst.html|title=Three Burgess brothers named in England World Cup squad|work=The Sydney Morning Herald|date=October 2013}} and played in 2 matches. ===2014=== Burgess returned to first grade in Round 11.{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/the-rabbitohs-have-beaten-the-sharks-180-at-remondis-stadium/story-e6frf3ou-1226932121823|title=The Rabbitohs have beaten the Sharks 18-0 at Remondis Stadium|author=Paul Crawley|date=8 March 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=9 March 2016}} In Round 21 against the [[Newcastle Knights]] at [[Barlow Park]] in [[Cairns, Queensland|Cairns]], Burgess scored his first NRL try in South Sydney's smashing 50-10 win.{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/south-sydney-rabbitohs-beat-newcastle-knights-5010-at-barlow-park-in-cairns/story-e6frf3ou-1227011840805|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs beat Newcastle Knights 50-10 at Barlow Park in Cairns|author=Barry Toohey|date=8 March 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=9 March 2016}} On 5 October, in South Sydney's [[2014 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]], Burgess played off the interchange bench in the club's 30-6 victory alongside his brothers [[George Burgess (rugby league)|George]] and [[Sam Burgess]].{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/rabbitohs-grand-final-player-ratings/tabid/10874/newsid/82375/default.aspx|title=Rabbitohs Grand Final Player Ratings|author=Dan Walsh|work=NRL.com|date=5 October 2014 }} Burgess finished the [[2014 NRL season|season]] with one try from 17 matches . Burgess was selected in the English final 24-man squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] series,{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/england-2014-four-nations-squad|title=ENGLAND NAME 2014 FOUR NATIONS SQUAD|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=4 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150103024433/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/england-2014-four-nations-squad/|archive-date=3 January 2015|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}} and played in all 3 matches of the tournament. [[File:Tom Burgess Rabbitohs.jpg|thumb|right|Tom Burgess playing for the Rabbitohs in 2019]] ===2015=== On 23 February, Burgess played for South Sydney in the [[2015 World Club Series|2015 World Club Challenge]] match against [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] premiers [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], playing off the interchange bench in the 39-0 win at [[Langtree Park]].{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/world-club-challenge-south-sydney-rabbitohs-thrash-st-helens-saints-to-complete-aussie-series-sweep/story-e6frf3uu-1227234753346|title=World Club Challenge: South Sydney Rabbitohs thrash St Helens Saints to complete Aussie series sweep|author=Mike Colman|date=8 March 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=9 March 2016}} Burgess extended his contract with the Rabbitohs to the end of the 2017 season after scoring 2 tries from his 24 appearances.{{cite web|work= nrl.com|title=Rabbitohs extend contracts of Burgess and Tyrrell|date=8 April 2015 |url=http://www.nrl.com/rabbitohs-extend-contracts-of-burgess-and-tyrrell/tabid/10874/newsid/85200/default.aspx}} Burgess scored his first international try in England's 84-4 shellacking win over [[France national rugby league team|France]] at [[Leigh, Greater Manchester|Leigh]].{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/live/match/43791/report|title=England demolish France 84-4 in record win|publisher=Skysports.com|access-date=24 October 2015}} He then played in the [[2015 New Zealand rugby league tour of Great Britain|end of year test series]] against [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]],{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/england-squad-named-for-nz-series/tabid/10874/newsid/90510/default.aspx|title=England squad named for NZ series|work=NRL.com|date=11 October 2015 }} appearing in all 3 matches off the interchange bench in England's 2-1 [[Baskerville Shield]] series win.{{cite news|work= Daily Telegraph|title=Hosts take series with nervy 20-14 win over Kiwis in Wigan|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/england-v-new-zealand-3rd-test-hosts-take-series-with-nervy-20-14-win-over-kiwis-in-wigan/story-fni3fbgz-1227609556526}} In December, Burgess trialled and trained with [[National Football League|NFL]] teams the [[Seattle Seahawks]] and [[Buffalo Bills]] after he attracted interest from the clubs.{{cite web|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/american-sports/report-sam-burgess-works-out-with-ny-giants/news-story/65886765dc501cfdb16f7a51f8b62cfb|title=Tom Burgess NFL: South Sydney Rabbitohs star at New York Giants|date=3 December 2015|work=NewsComAu}}{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsports.com.au/nfl/south-sydney-rugby-league-star-tom-burgess-works-out-with-new-york-giants/story-fnp3l4e4-1227633458873|title=South Sydney rugby league star Tom Burgess works out with New York Giants|author=Melanie Dinjaski|date=8 March 2016|work=Fox Sports|access-date=9 March 2016}} ===2016=== In February 2016, Burgess played in South Sydney's Auckland Nines squad,{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/rabbitohs-name-2016-nines-squad|title=Rabbitohs name 2016 Nines squad - Rugby League Week|work=Rugby League Week|access-date=9 March 2016|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160320182624/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/rabbitohs-name-2016-nines-squad/|archive-date=20 March 2016|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}} On 13 February 2016, Burgess played for the [[World All Stars]] against the [[Indigenous All Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]] in the [[2016 All Stars match]], starting at prop in the 12-8 win at [[Suncorp Stadium]].{{Cite web |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/world-all-stars-defeat-indigenous-all-stars-128-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/034a63c6602597828b4b9a5ce72f986e |title=World All Stars defeat Indigenous All Stars 12-8 at Suncorp Stadium | Daily Telegraph |access-date=16 February 2016 |archive-date=4 February 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220204200333/https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/world-all-stars-defeat-indigenous-all-stars-128-at-suncorp-stadium/news-story/034a63c6602597828b4b9a5ce72f986e |url-status=dead }} After missing the first 4 rounds due to injury, in Round 5 against the [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]], Burgess made his return to the South Sydney team, playing off the interchange bench in the 16-12 win at [[Brookvale Oval]].{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/south-sydney-rabbitohs/south-sydney-rabbitohs-sam-burgess-back-to-play-manly-sea-eagles-after-neck-injury-scare-20160328-gnss57.html|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs' Sam Burgess back to play Manly Sea Eagles after neck injury scare|date=29 March 2016|access-date=9 October 2017|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]}} In July 2016, it was rumoured that Burgess was being offloaded to other clubs in a clean-out overhaul in their underperforming roster.{{cite news|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/south-sydney-rabbitohs-attempting-to-offload-prop-tom-burgess-20160727-gqf88w.html|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs attempting to offload prop Tom Burgess|first=Adrian|last=Proszenko|date=27 July 2016|access-date=9 October 2017|newspaper=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]]}} Burgess finished the [[2016 NRL season]] with him playing in 19 matches for South Sydney. At the end of the season, Tom was selected in England's 24-man squad for the [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations|2016 Four Nations]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/four-nations/four-nations-2016-wayne-bennett-names-24man-england-squad/news-story/e7858c8f7346c8340b79248f839150aa|title=Four Nations 2016: Wayne Bennett's England squad|date=10 October 2016|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=9 October 2017}} Before the tournament began, England played a [[2016 Rugby League Four Nations#France vs England|test match]] against France in which Burgess scored his second international try in England's 40-6 win.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/four-nations/live-four-nations-warm-up-match-france-v-england/news-story/3f3738a3c19b1dd18f30d544a40aab39|title=England v France rugby league Test match live: Four Nations warm up live scores, live updates|date=23 October 2016|work=[[Fox Sports]]|access-date=9 October 2017}} Burgess played in all 3 matches off the interchange bench of the tournament. ===2017=== On 6 May 2017, Burgess played for England in the test against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]], playing off the interchange bench in the 30-10 win at [[Campbelltown Stadium]].{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/james-graham-josh-hodgson-have-led-england-to-victory-over-samoa/news-story/4126c6c23ef7acc51890a3b0e03479d9|title=NRL stars deliver for Bennett's England|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=9 October 2017}} Burgess finished the [[2017 NRL season]] with him playing in 22 matches for the South Sydney club. In October 2017 he was selected in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}} Burgess played in The World Cup final against Australia and made two crucial errors towards the end of the game in which Australia came out victorious 6-0. On 6 December 2017, Burgess signed an extension contract with Souths keeping him at the club until the end of 2019.{{cite web|url=http://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/watch-did-tom-burgess-just-drop-the-world-cuptwice/14a59fpokpbuf16yy7d7umf1ru|title=WATCH: Did Tom Burgess just drop the World Cup…twice?|date=2 December 2017|access-date=15 June 2018}}{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-market-watch-souths-burgess-signing-drive-alex-walmsley-price-dylan-napa-latest/news-story/33bbb83837cfd51ef461a46db48e7a3f|title=NRL contracts 2018: Sam Burgess among Rabbitohs signings, Alex Walmsley, Dylan Napa — Market Watch|date=5 December 2017|access-date=15 June 2018}} ===2018=== Burgess scored his first try in two years against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in round 11 in South Sydney's 30-10 victory at [[Mount Smart Stadium]]. The following week he scored a double against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] at [[ANZ Stadium]] in South Sydney's 22-14 win. Burgess made 27 appearances for Souths in total as the club finished 3rd on the table at the end of the regular season and reached the preliminary final before being defeated by eventual premiers the [[Sydney Roosters]] 12-4.{{cite web|url=http://nothingbutleague.com/2018/10/16/2018-season-review-south-sydney-rabbitohs/|title=South Sydney season review|website=Nothing but league|date=16 October 2018 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTItMTcxNy00LS0tLS0tMTY4ODktLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t/|title=Custom Matches|website=RLP}} [[File:Thomas Burgess.jpg|thumb|right|Burgess warming up for England in 2022]] In 2018 he was selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]] against [[France national rugby league team|France]] at the [[Leigh Sports Village]].{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2018/10/17/tom-johnstone-marks-debut-england-hat-trick-against-france/|title=Tom Johnstone marks debut with England hat-trick against France|newspaper=Guardian|date=17 October 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}} ===2019=== Burgess began the [[2019 NRL season]] in good form as the club won 10 of their first 11 games to sit on top of the table. Following South Sydney's Round 13 loss against [[Newcastle Knights]], Burgess was ruled out of action for 4–6 weeks with an ankle injury. Burgess returned to the Souths side in Round 19 which South Sydney won 20-16 after the final siren had sounded.{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/07/26/rabbitohs-steal-last-second-win-over-brave-dragons/|title=Rabbitohs steal last-second win over brave Dragons|website=NRL|date=26 July 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/sam-burgess-tom-burgess-injury-south-sydney-rabbitohs/fw1aygqs1bjy1edqwl4uhsokj/|title=Tom Burgess joins Sam in the casualty ward|website=Sporting News|date=14 August 2021 }} Burgess made a total of 19 appearances for South Sydney in the [[2019 NRL season]] as the club finished 3rd on the table and qualified for the finals. Burgess featured in all 3 of the club's finals games as they reached the preliminary final against [[Canberra Raiders]], but were defeated 16-10 at [[Canberra Stadium]].{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-09-20/nrl-south-sydney-rabbitohs-beat-manly-sea-eagles-in-semi-final/11527098/|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs beat Manly Sea Eagles 34-26 in NRL semi-final|newspaper=ABC News|date=20 September 2019 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/09/20/rabbitohs-dig-deep-to-eliminate-sea-eagles-in-finals-thriller/|title=Rabbitohs dig deep to eliminate Sea Eagles in finals thriller|website=NRL|date=20 September 2019 }}{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2019/sep/27/canberra-raiders-end-25-year-wait-to-reach-nrl-grand-final-with-victory-over-rabbitohs/|title=Canberra Raiders end 25-year wait to reach NRL grand final with victory over Rabbitohs|website=The Guardian|date=27 September 2019 }} On 10 October, Burgess was named in the England team for the [[2019 Rugby League World Cup 9s]].{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sam-tomkins-eager-lift-inaugural-20595184|title=Sam Tomkins eager to lift inaugural World 9s title with England Down Under|publisher=Mirror|date=16 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}} He was selected in squad for the [[2019 Great Britain Lions tour]] of the Southern Hemisphere.{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/rugby-league/2019/10/14/zak-hardaker-shock-inclusion-24-man-great-britain-squad-tour/|title=Zak Hardaker shock inclusion in 24-man Great Britain squad for tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea|newspaper=Telegraph|date=14 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}} He made his [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] test debut in the defeat by [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]].{{cite news|url=https://www.thetimes.com/article/great-britain-tonga-rugby-league-hzmmhr5q5|title=Great Britain left bruised at Tonga party|newspaper=Times|date=26 October 2019|access-date=29 October 2019}} ===2020=== Burgess played 23 games for Souths throughout the year as the club reached their third straight preliminary final. However, for the third straight year Souths would fall short of a grand final appearance losing to Penrith 20-16 at [[ANZ Stadium]].{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-10-17/nrl-preliminary-final-penrith-panthers-south-sydney-scorecentre/12774550/|title=Penrith Panthers beat South Sydney Rabbitohs 20-16 to set up NRL grand final against Melbourne Storm|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 October 2020 }} ===2021=== In round 6 of the [[2021 NRL season]], he scored the winning try for South Sydney in their 18-14 golden point extra-time victory over the [[Wests Tigers]].{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-04-17/nrl-eels-canberra-rabbitohs-wests-tigers-sea-eagles-titans/100072470/|title=Parramatta beats Canberra 35-10 as South Sydney, Manly post contrasting NRL wins|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 April 2021 }} In round 19, he scored two tries for Souths in their 60-22 victory over the [[New Zealand Warriors]].{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-07-24/nrl-live-souths-warriors-manly-tigers-penrith-broncos-scores/100308594/|title=Penrith beat Brisbane 18-12, South Sydney thrash Warriors 60-22, Manly outclass Tigers 44-24|newspaper=ABC News|date=24 July 2021 }} Burgess played a total of 26 games for South Sydney in the [[2021 NRL season]] including the club's [[2021 NRL Grand Final]] defeat against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/oct/04/one-of-finest-nrl-grand-finals-of-all-time-provides-cure-to-difficult-season/|title=One of finest NRL grand finals of all time provides cure to difficult season|newspaper=The Guardian|date=3 October 2021 |last1=Tedeschi |first1=Nick }} ===2022=== In round 20 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Burgess was sent off for a dangerous high tackle in South Sydney's 21-20 loss against [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-cronulla-sharks-vs-south-sydney-rabbitohs-live-stream-live-blog-supercoach-scores-videos-latrell-mitchell-nicho-hynes/news-story/1ea1a6488e2bceccbd3e2e9fcb553f46/|title=Cool Hynes ices golden point epic; Latrell's mixed bag as Souths rue missed chances: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=30 July 2022 }} In the first week of the finals, Burgess was sent to the sin bin for a dangerous high tackle in South Sydney's 30-14 upset victory over arch-rivals the [[Sydney Roosters]].{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2022-sin-bin-punch-sydney-roosters-victor-radley-south-sydney-rabbitohs-taane-milne-thomas-burgess-ashley-klein-video-high-tackle/news-story/769cf6e583f605ae656c0617f5064469/|title=SEVEN players binned in historic first after Roosters-Rabbitohs clash explodes|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=13 September 2022 }} On 13 September, Burgess was suspended for two matches over his high tackle on [[James Tedesco]].{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-09-13/tom-burgess-suspension-south-sydney-rabbitohs/101434924/|title=South Sydney take aim at NRL double-standard following Tom Burgess suspension|newspaper=ABC News|date=13 September 2022 }} In October he was named in the [[2021_Men's_Rugby_League_World_Cup_squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup – Men's tournament|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021] In the third group stage match at the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup]], Burgess scored two tries for England in a 94-4 victory over Greece.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63439484|title=Rugby League World Cup: England crush Greece as Dom Young scores four tries|work=BBC Sport }} In November he was named in the [[Template:2021 RLWC Team of the Tournament|2021 RLWC Team of the Tournament]].[https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-team-of-the-tournament-samoan-players-matt-parish-jarome-luai-mens-news-grand-final-australia-vs-samoa-news/news-story/a21b36bd8c3bf810be6953e4420220a9 Samoan domination as unlikely halfback earns No.7 jersey: WC Team of the Tournament] ===2023=== In round 23 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Burgess was placed on report during South Sydney's 26-16 loss against Cronulla. On 6 August, Burgess was controversially suspended for three matches with many critics saying the length of the suspension too harsh.{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/england-star-tom-burgess-set-to-miss-rest-of-season-following-harsh-sin-bin-79743/|title=England star Tom Burgess set to miss rest of season following 'harsh' sin bin|website=www.seriousaboutrl.com|date=6 August 2023 }}{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-tom-burgess-sin-bin-for-tackle-on-tom-hazleton-south-sydney-rabbitohs-vs-cronulla-sharks-round-23/news-story/dd415a8a8bf6915c15e5c01f5765594a/|title=Burgess hit with monster ban for contentious Hazleton hit amid 'harsh' sin bin|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=6 August 2023 }} Burgess played a total of 16 games throughout the year as South Sydney finished 9th on the table and missed the finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-south-sydney-rabbitohs-latrell-mitchell-changing-position-centres-halves-fullback-jack-wighton-sam-burgess-2024-jason-demetriou/news-story/d7c72c901f9e61a0b76b55f9162b64cd/|title='Absolute madness': Potential Latrell switch slammed as a 'band aid' for broken Bunnies|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=5 September 2023 }} ===2024=== On 7 February, it was announced that Burgess had signed a three-year deal with English side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] which is due to start in the 2025 Super League season.{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/tom-burgess-super-league-move-confirmed-for-2025-as-england-international-set-to-join-huddersfield-giants/|title=Tom Burgess' Super League move confirmed for 2025 as England international set to join Huddersfield Giants|website=www.totalrl.com}} In round 8 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Burgess became South Sydney's second most capped player in their history. South Sydney would go on to lose 54–20 against Melbourne. In his final game for South Sydney in round 27, Burgess scored a try and kicked a goal in the club's 36–28 loss against arch-rivals the Sydney Roosters.{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2024-04-25/nrl-anzac-day-warriors-titans-dragons-roosters-storm-rabbitohs/103764148/|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs' Anzac Day loss to Melbourne Storm made worse by Taane Milne's 'brain explosion' on Cameron Munster|website=www.abc.net.au}} ===2025=== In round 1 of the 2025 Super League season, Burgess made his club debut for Huddersfield in their 20-12 loss against Warrington.{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5y746m3n8po|title=Warrington fight back to seal win at Huddersfield|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} Burgess played 22 matches for Huddersfield in the 2025 Super League season as the club finished 10th on the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/huddersfield-giants-coach-sounds-nrl-warning-as-honest-admission-made-over-2025-shortcomings/|title=Huddersfield Giants coach sounds NRL warning as honest
